name: Master Service Agreement v4.2.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-010  Master Service Agreement v4.2
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Master Service Agreement v4.2
Counterparty draft used by Meridian for legal review and workflow testing.
Document ID
CTR-010
Owner
Legal / Procurement
Status
Counterparty Draft — Under Review
Effective Date
2024-01-15
Customer
Meridian Holdings Ltd.
Counterparty
Northwind Cloud Systems LLC
Contract Value
USD 2,400,000 annually
Governing Law
State of Delaware
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
1. Parties, Scope, and Order of Precedence
This Master Service Agreement (the Agreement) is entered into by and between Meridian Holdings Ltd. (Customer) and
Northwind Cloud Systems LLC (Provider) for managed cloud retrieval, analytics acceleration, and knowledge-operations
support services used by Meridian enterprise teams.
The Agreement governs production and pre-production services ordered under written service orders. Applicable service
levels are set out in the Cloud Infrastructure SLA, and personal-data obligations are addressed in the Data Processing
Addendum 2024. In the event of inconsistency, the more specific document governs only for the subject matter it expressly
covers.
Commercial Schedule
Field
Draft Position
Initial Term
36 months starting 2024-01-15
Renewal
Automatic 12-month renewals unless notice is delivered 90 days before the renewal date
Annual Contract Value
USD 2,400,000 excluding approved overages and taxes
Primary Services
Managed retrieval cluster, secure API gateway, support coverage, analytics observability
Order of Precedence
MSA → Service Order → Cloud Infrastructure SLA → Data Processing Addendum
2. Security, Service, and Audit Support
Clause 2.4 — Reasonable Security Measures. Provider shall maintain administrative, technical, and physical safeguards
designed to protect Customer Data against unauthorized access, loss, or misuse, using security measures consistent with
generally accepted industry practice.
Clause 2.7 — Incident Cooperation. Provider shall use commercially reasonable efforts to investigate security events
affecting the Services and shall keep Customer reasonably informed of material developments.
Clause 2.9 — Audit Materials. Provider may satisfy diligence requests by providing a current certification package,
executive summary, or other summary materials selected by Provider. Customer shall not have an on-site or remote audit

[Page 2]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-010  Master Service Agreement v4.2
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 2
right unless required by non-waivable law.
Review cue: clauses 2.7 and 2.9 are intentionally softer than Meridian policy and baseline language. They
are suitable targets for contract-gap extraction and recommendation workflows.
3. Data Use and Intellectual Property
Clause 3.2 — Service Data. Provider may collect and use service telemetry, diagnostic output, and de-identified usage
information to operate, secure, improve, and train Provider service features, provided that Provider does not intentionally
identify Customer personnel in such data.
Clause 3.4 — Derivatives and Improvements. Any model refinements, derived schemas, prompt patterns, runbooks, or
automation artifacts developed jointly in connection with the Services shall be jointly owned by the parties, and each party
may use such derivatives without further consent or accounting to the other party.

[Page 3]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
CTR-010  Master Service Agreement v4.2
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 3
4. Liability and Indemnification
Clause 7.1 — Exclusion of Certain Damages. Neither party will be liable for lost profits, revenue, goodwill, or indirect,
incidental, special, exemplary, or consequential damages, except to the extent such damages are awarded to a third party
under an indemnified claim.
Clause 7.3 — Unlimited Liability. Each party shall be liable to the other for all losses, damages, costs, and expenses
arising out of or related to this Agreement, and no monetary cap or aggregate limit shall apply unless expressly stated in the
applicable service order.
Clause 7.5 — Indemnification. Provider shall defend and indemnify Customer from third-party claims alleging
intellectual-property infringement or unauthorized disclosure of Customer Data caused by Provider. Customer shall defend
and indemnify Provider for claims arising from Customer materials or configurations supplied by Customer. Neither party
will be responsible for consequential damages outside this section.
Clause 7.8 — Limitation Period. Any claim arising under or related to this Agreement must be brought within twelve (12)
months after the event giving rise to the claim, regardless of any contrary statute of limitations.
Observed Risk Signals
●
Section 7.3 creates uncapped financial exposure because it contains no liability ceiling for general commercial claims.
●
Section 7.5 does not clearly align remedies with the Data Processing Addendum or the supplier code of conduct.
●
Section 7.8 shortens the claim window to 12 months, which is tighter than Meridian’s approved 24-month fallback for
strategic vendors.
5. Termination, Renewal, and General Terms
Clause 8.1 — Initial Term. The initial term is thirty-six (36) months unless terminated earlier in accordance with this
Agreement.
Clause 8.2 — Automatic Renewal. Following the initial term, the Agreement renews automatically for successive
twelve-month periods unless either party delivers written notice of non-renewal at least ninety (90) days before the end of
the then-current term.
Clause 8.4 — Termination for Convenience. Neither party may terminate this Agreement for convenience during the
initial term.
Clause 9.6 — Governing Law. This Agreement is governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to
conflicts-of-law principles.
Revision History
Version
Date
Owner
Summary
4.0
2023-11-27
Northwind Legal
Introduced shared-derivative language and revised renewal
mechanics.
4.1
2023-12-19
Northwind Legal
Added 12-month claims limitation and narrowed audit support
language.
4.2
2024-01-12
Northwind Legal
Current draft issued for Meridian review.
Review Routing Hints
●
Extract: Contracting Party, Effective Date, Annual Value, Term Duration, Governing Law, Liability Cap, Notice Period.
●
Findings: unlimited liability, joint derivative IP, 90-day auto-renewal, short limitation period.
●
Comparison anchors: liability cap, IP ownership, notice period, audit rights, data-use language.
